mappings.rationaleAI: The Empire of Mali from its founding by Sundiata Keita, its control of trans-Saharan gold and salt trade, Mansa Musa's pilgrimage, and Timbuktu as a centre of Islamic learning directly correspond to B.2.a on the Keita dynasty and Timbuktu as cultural and commercial centre.

The Almoravid Empire's conquest of Ghana and the gold trade directly corresponds to B.1 on the development of West African monarchies of Ghana, as the Almoravids' intervention transformed the political landscape.
The Zong Massacre (1781) and its significance for the abolitionist movement directly correspond to C.2 on the Atlantic slave trade and C.4.a on European antislavery movements.

The Berlin Conference of 1884-85 and the Scramble for Africa, including the arbitrary drawing of borders, directly correspond to C.4.b on British colonial settlements and the broader European colonization context circa 1885.

The transatlantic slave trade, British involvement, and the abolitionist movement from Wilberforce to emancipation directly correspond to C.2 on the rise of the Atlantic slave trade and C.4.a on antislavery movements.

mappings.episodeTitle: The Zulu Nation's Rise and Fall
mappings.rationaleAI: While focused on southern Africa, the Zulu Nation's mfecane caused widespread upheaval affecting peoples across the subcontinent, providing comparative context for C.4 on West Africa from 1800 to c. 1885.
